topics.title: Scalability Basics
topics.overview: Understanding horizontal and vertical scaling, load balancing, and distributed systems fundamentals.
topics.architecture: Distribute load across multiple servers using load balancers to handle increased traffic and data.
topics.realWorldExamples: Netflix uses horizontal scaling with thousands of servers across regions. Facebook distributes load using consistent hashing.
topics.interviewTips: Discuss trade-offs between horizontal and vertical scaling. Mention capacity planning and auto-scaling strategies.

topics.title: Database Design
topics.overview: SQL vs NoSQL, sharding, replication, and consistency models for different use cases.
topics.architecture: Choose database type based on data structure, consistency requirements, and scale needs.
topics.realWorldExamples: Amazon uses DynamoDB for NoSQL needs and RDS for relational data. Google uses BigTable for massive scale.
topics.interviewTips: Explain ACID vs BASE. Discuss when to use SQL vs NoSQL. Cover partitioning strategies and replication.

topics.title: Caching Strategies
topics.overview: Cache invalidation, LRU cache, distributed caching with Redis, and cache-aside patterns.
topics.architecture: Implement multi-level caching: application level, database query cache, HTTP cache, and CDN.
topics.realWorldExamples: Instagram uses Redis for caching user feeds. Twitter uses memcached for timeline caching.
topics.interviewTips: Discuss cache invalidation strategies: TTL, event-based, write-through. Mention cache stampede and solutions.

topics.title: Message Queues
topics.overview: Understanding queues, pub-sub systems, and async processing for decoupled architecture.
topics.architecture: Decouple services using message queues for asynchronous communication and event-driven architecture.
topics.realWorldExamples: Uber uses Kafka for event streaming. Amazon uses SQS for message queuing across services.
topics.interviewTips: Discuss ordering guarantees, exactly-once delivery, and failure handling. Mention dead letter queues.

topics.title: API Design
topics.overview: RESTful APIs, rate limiting, versioning, pagination, and API gateways.
topics.architecture: Design stateless APIs with proper versioning, rate limiting, and caching headers.
topics.realWorldExamples: GitHub API uses semantic versioning. Stripe implements comprehensive rate limiting and webhooks.
topics.interviewTips: Discuss pagination strategies (offset, cursor). Explain rate limiting algorithms (token bucket, sliding window).

topics.title: Microservices
topics.overview: Service-oriented architecture, containerization with Docker, orchestration with Kubernetes.
topics.architecture: Break monolith into small, independent services with clear responsibilities and communication protocols.
topics.realWorldExamples: Netflix pioneered microservices. Amazon uses thousands of microservices. Airbnb uses service mesh.
topics.interviewTips: Discuss service discovery, circuit breakers, and distributed tracing. Mention Kubernetes orchestration benefits.

topics.title: Search Systems
topics.overview: Full-text search, inverted indexes, and search engine architecture.
topics.architecture: Implement distributed search using Elasticsearch or similar, with indexing and query optimization.
topics.realWorldExamples: Airbnb uses Elasticsearch for search. Google uses proprietary search engine with distributed indexing.
topics.interviewTips: Discuss inverted indexes and ranking algorithms. Mention relevance scoring and faceted search.

topics.title: CDN and Content Delivery
topics.overview: Content delivery networks, edge servers, and geographic distribution.
topics.architecture: Distribute content across global edge locations to reduce latency and improve availability.
topics.realWorldExamples: Netflix uses CDN for video delivery. Cloudflare provides CDN and security. AWS CloudFront is widely used.
topics.interviewTips: Discuss geographic routing and cache invalidation. Mention purge strategies and versioning.
